dispel_type.brief: Defines the properties and messaging behavior of a magical dispel action within the system.
dispel_type.details: The dispel_type struct models the characteristics of a magical dispel operation, including its capabilities and communication aspects. It contains boolean flags such as can_undo, which indicates if the dispel can be reversed; can_cancel, which specifies if the dispel can be canceled by the entity itself; and can_dispel, denoting whether the dispel can remove effects from others using 'dispel magic'. Additionally, it holds a message string (msg_to_room) that is broadcasted to the room to notify participants of the dispel event. This design allows for flexible configuration of dispel behaviors, supporting various gameplay mechanics and ensuring consistent messaging across different scenarios.
dispel_type.notes: This struct employs a straightforward data encapsulation pattern, with boolean flags controlling behavior and a message string for communication. It is used in conjunction with game mechanics that handle magical effects and their dispelling, enabling modular and clear management of dispel actions.
dispel_type.rationale: The dispel_type struct exists to standardize and manage the properties of dispel actions within the game system. By encapsulating capabilities and messaging, it facilitates consistent behavior and communication for dispel mechanics, supporting maintainability and extensibility in the game's architecture.
dispel_type.name: dispel_type

can_undo.brief: Flag indicating whether the dispel type supports undo operations.
can_undo.details: The `can_undo` boolean variable determines if a specific dispel type allows its effects to be reversed or undone by the user. It is set during initialization based on the capabilities of the dispel method and is used throughout the codebase to control whether undo actions are permitted for this dispel type. This variable interacts with user interface elements and game logic to ensure that undo functionality is only available when supported, maintaining consistent behavior across different dispel effects.
can_undo.notes: Typically set during the creation of a dispel_type instance, this flag influences undo-related decision-making and user interactions.
can_undo.rationale: This variable exists to enable or disable undo functionality for different dispel types, ensuring that only supported dispels can be reversed, which helps maintain game consistency and prevents unsupported undo operations.
can_undo.name: can_undo

can_cancel.brief: Flag indicating whether this dispel type can be canceled directly by the entity itself.
can_cancel.details: The `can_cancel` variable determines if the dispel effect can be canceled by the entity it is associated with, allowing for dynamic control over dispel lifecycle. It is typically initialized based on the properties of the dispel effect and is checked during gameplay or processing to enforce cancellation rules. This flag interacts with other control mechanisms to manage effect termination and ensures consistent behavior when canceling dispel effects, supporting flexible gameplay mechanics.
can_cancel.notes: This boolean is stored as a simple flag and is crucial for controlling whether an entity has the ability to cancel its own dispel effects, affecting effect management and user interactions.
can_cancel.rationale: The variable exists to facilitate conditional cancellation of dispel effects, enabling entities to have control over their own effects and ensuring proper handling of dispel lifecycle events in various gameplay scenarios.
can_cancel.name: can_cancel

can_dispel.brief: Indicates whether the entity has the ability to dispel magical effects on others using "dispel magic".
can_dispel.details: The `dispel_type::can_dispel` boolean variable specifies if the entity can perform dispel actions on other entities via the "dispel magic" spell. It is typically set during initialization based on the entity's traits, abilities, or class features. During gameplay, this flag is checked to determine if the entity is permitted to attempt dispelling effects, influencing interactions with magical effects and status conditions. The variable works in conjunction with other dispel-related flags and mechanics to control dispelling behavior within the game system.
can_dispel.notes: This variable is stored as a simple boolean, allowing quick checks during gameplay. Its value may vary depending on the entity's current state or buffs that temporarily grant or revoke dispelling capabilities.
can_dispel.rationale: The variable exists to clearly indicate whether an entity can perform dispel actions, enabling consistent and efficient handling of dispelling mechanics across different entity types and game scenarios.
can_dispel.name: can_dispel

msg_to_room.brief: The message content to be sent to a specific room.
msg_to_room.details: This variable holds the message that will be dispatched to a designated room within the system. It is initialized with the message text during the setup or processing phase and is used whenever a broadcast or notification needs to be delivered to all occupants of that room. The String class used for this variable extends standard string functionality, providing features such as case-insensitive comparisons and advanced manipulation methods, which can facilitate message filtering or formatting tasks.
msg_to_room.notes: The String class's extended features may be leveraged for processing the message content, such as case-insensitive searches or modifications before sending.
msg_to_room.rationale: This variable exists to store the specific message that should be communicated to all users in a room, supporting consistent and efficient message broadcasting within the system.
msg_to_room.name: msg_to_room
